Design philosophy: **open and see your data in under three seconds** — your pinned stations front and center, no map to dismiss, no search to type.\n4\t\n5\tVersion 0.0.1\n6\t\n7\t## Preview\n8\t\n9\tScreenshots are available in `docs/`.\n10\t\n11\t## Features\n12\t\n13\t1. **Favorites-first home screen** — pinned stations with inline live arrival countdowns; GPS-powered 60-second onboarding for first-time users\n14\t2. **Real-time arrivals** — live countdowns per station, both directions; pull-to-refresh; 30s auto-refresh; offline fallback via PWA cache\n15\t3. **Transfer intelligence** — commute analyzer shows whether you should transfer using real-time train positions: RECOMMENDED / DIRECT / ALSO POSSIBLE, with walk-time comparison\n16\t4. **Predictive delay detection** — tracks vehicle positions across consecutive 30s polls; when a train's inter-station time exceeds 2× the scheduled baseline, generates early-warning synthetic alerts before MTA publishes them\n17\t5. **Smart alerts** — filtered to your exact stations, lines, and directions; plain-language rewrites of raw MTA alert text; push notifications via WebPush/VAPID\n18\t6. **Context-aware switching** — detects home/commute/transfer context from location, time patterns, and tap history; frequency-sorts favorites during commute hours\n19\t7. **Interactive transit map** — SVG map with pan/zoom, real-time pulsing train positions, line filtering, and tap-to-detail modal\n20\t8. **Trip journal** — automatic trip tracking with full history and statistics (average, median, std dev, trends)\n21\t9. **Subway Year** — shareable annual summary card (Spotify Wrapped-style), exportable as PNG; configurable time window\n22\t10. **Fare tracking** — OMNY fare cap progress and weekly spending\n23\t11. **Elevator/escalator status** — equipment status per station from the MTA ENE feed\n24\t12. **Health dashboard** — `/status` HTML page and `/api/health` JSON endpoint with per-feed circuit-breaker state\n25\t\n26\t## Transit Data\n27\t\n28\tAll real-time data is from the [MTA GTFS-RT Protobuf API](https://api.mta.info/). An API key is optional but recommended to avoid rate limiting — register free at https://api.mta.info/\n29\t\n30\t| Feed | Lines |\n31\t|------|-------|\n32\t| gtfs | 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 S GS |\n33\t| gtfs-ace | A C E H FS |\n34\t| gtfs-bdfm | B D F M |\n35\t| gtfs-g | G |\n36\t| gtfs-jz | J Z |\n37\t| gtfs-l | L |\n38\t| gtfs-nqrw | N Q R W |\n39\t| gtfs-si | SIR (Staten Island Railway) |\n40\t\n41\tPolling intervals: 30s (arrivals), 60s (alerts), 300s (equipment). Static schedule from MTA's published GTFS ZIP.\n42\t\n43\t## Quick Start\n44\t\n45\t**Prerequisites:** Node.js ≥ 22, npm ≥ 10\n46\t\n47\t```bash\n48\tgit clone https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way.git\n49\tcd mta-my-way\n50\tnpm install\n51\t\n52\t# One-time: process GTFS static schedule data\n53\tnpm run process-gtfs --workspace=packages/server\n54\t\n55\t# Start development server\n56\tnpm run dev --workspace=packages/server\n57\t```\n58\t\n59\tApp available at `http://localhost:3001`. Set `MTA_API_KEY` in your environment to avoid MTA rate limits during development.\n60\t\n61\t## Docker\n62\t\n63\t```bash\n64\tdocker build -t mta-my-way .\n65\tdocker run \\\n66\t  -e ALLOWED_HOSTS=your.domain.com \\\n67\t  -e MTA_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\n68\t  -p 3000:3000 \\\n69\t  mta-my-way\n70\t```\n71\t\n72\tThe container exposes port 3000.\n73\t\n74\t## Environment Variables\n75\t\n76\t| Variable | Required | Default | Notes |\n77\t|----------|----------|---------|-------|\n78\t| `PORT` | No | `3000` | HTTP listen port |\n79\t| `NODE_ENV` | No | — | `production` or `development` |\n80\t| `ALLOWED_HOSTS` | Yes (prod) | — | Comma-separated hostnames; server refuses to start without this in production |\n81\t| `MTA_API_KEY` | Recommended | — | MTA GTFS-RT API key; works without but may be rate-limited. Register at https://api.mta.info/ |\n82\t| `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY` | For push | — | Generate: `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys` |\n83\t| `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` | For push | — | Never commit to version control |\n84\t| `VAPID_SUBJECT` | For push | `mailto:mta-my-way@example.com` | Contact URI included in push requests |\n85\t| `PASSWORD_PEPPER` | Recommended | — | `openssl rand -hex 32` |\n86\t| `EMAIL_PROVIDER` | No | `console` | `ses`, `smtp`, `sendgrid`, or `console` |\n87\t| `EMAIL_FROM` | No | `noreply@mtamyway.com` | Sender address for transactional email |\n88\t| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `info` | `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |\n89\t\n90\t## Development Commands\n91\t\n92\t```bash\n93\tnpm test                        # all unit/integration tests (Vitest)\n94\tnpm run test:watch              # watch mode\n95\tcd tests/e2e && npm test        # Playwright E2E tests\n96\tnpm run lint                    # Biome + ESLint\n97\tnpm run format                  # Biome format --write\n98\tnpm run typecheck               # tsc --build\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\t**Code style:** Biome for formatting, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Commit prefix convention: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`.\n102\t\n103\t## API Reference\n104\t\n105\t| Method | Path | Description |\n106\t|--------|------|-------------|\n107\t| GET | `/status` | Health dashboard (HTML) |\n108\t| GET | `/api/health` | Per-feed status and circuit-breaker state (JSON) |\n109\t| GET | `/api/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |\n110\t| GET | `/api/arrivals/:stationId` | Real-time arrivals for a station |\n111\t| GET | `/api/stations` | Full GTFS station list |\n112\t| GET | `/api/stations/search` | Type-ahead search by name, line, or cross-street |\n113\t| GET | `/api/routes` | Route index |\n114\t| POST | `/api/commute/analyze` | Analyze routes between origin and destination |\n115\t| GET | `/api/alerts` | All current MTA alerts |\n116\t| GET | `/api/alerts/:lineId` | Alerts filtered by line |\n117\t| GET | `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | VAPID public key for push registration |\n118\t| POST | `/api/push/subscribe` | Register device for push notifications |\n119\t| GET | `/api/trip/:tripId` | Live trip progress (stop-by-stop) |\n120\t\n121\t## Observability\n122\t\n123\tOpenTelemetry (OTLP gRPC), Prometheus `/api/metrics`, Pino structured JSON logging. See [docs/observability.md](docs/observability.md) for configuration details.\n124\t\n125\t## Project Structure\n126\t\n127\t```\n128\tmta-my-way/\n129\t├── packages/\n130\t│   ├── shared/           # TypeScript types and constants (feed config, polling intervals)\n131\t│   ├── server/\n132\t│   │   ├── src/          # Hono app, pollers, GTFS parsers, push engine, context engine\n133\t│   │   ├── data/         # Generated GTFS JSON + SQLite (subscriptions, trips, sessions)\n134\t│   │   └── scripts/      # process-gtfs.mjs\n135\t│   └── web/\n136\t│       └── src/\n137\t│           ├── screens/  # Home, Station, Map, Commute, Alerts, Journal, Stats, ...\n138\t│           ├── components/\n139\t│           ├── hooks/\n140\t│           └── stores/   # Zustand (favorites, journal, settings, fare)\n141\t├── tests/\n142\t│   └── e2e/              # Playwright tests\n143\t├── docs/\n144\t│   ├── plan/plan.md      # Architecture and roadmap\n145\t│   ├── testing.md\n146\t│   ├── security.md\n147\t│   └── observability.md\n148\t└── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: build-web → build-server → runtime\n149\t```\n150\t\n151\t## Documentation\n152\t\n153\t- [Testing Guide](docs/testing.md)\n154\t- [Security](docs/security.md)\n155\t- [Observability](docs/observability.md)\n156\t- [Architecture Plan](docs/plan/plan.md)\n157\t\n158\t## License\n159\t\n160\tMIT\n161\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e14e0169-84cf-4b43-9dcc-5d36fa70a675","uuid":"e94c867a-1862-4e8b-a237-299bab9d0bc3","timestamp":"2026-07-04T09:51:38.299Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/README.md","content":"# MTA My Way\n\nA mobile-first Progressive Web App (PWA) for daily NYC subway commuters. Design philosophy: **open and see your data in under three seconds** — your pinned stations front and center, no map to dismiss, no search to type.\n\nVersion 0.0.1\n\n## Preview\n\nScreenshots are available in `docs/`.\n\n## Features\n\n1. **Favorites-first home screen** — pinned stations with inline live arrival countdowns; GPS-powered 60-second onboarding for first-time users\n2. **Real-time arrivals** — live countdowns per station, both directions; pull-to-refresh; 30s auto-refresh; offline fallback via PWA cache\n3. **Transfer intelligence** — commute analyzer shows whether you should transfer using real-time train positions: RECOMMENDED / DIRECT / ALSO POSSIBLE, with walk-time comparison\n4. **Predictive delay detection** — tracks vehicle positions across consecutive 30s polls; when a train's inter-station time exceeds 2× the scheduled baseline, generates early-warning synthetic alerts before MTA publishes them\n5. **Smart alerts** — filtered to your exact stations, lines, and directions; plain-language rewrites of raw MTA alert text; push notifications via WebPush/VAPID\n6. **Context-aware switching** — detects home/commute/transfer context from location, time patterns, and tap history; frequency-sorts favorites during commute hours\n7. **Interactive transit map** — SVG map with pan/zoom, real-time pulsing train positions, line filtering, and tap-to-detail modal\n8. **Trip journal** — automatic trip tracking with full history and statistics (average, median, std dev, trends)\n9. **Subway Year** — shareable annual summary card (Spotify Wrapped-style), exportable as PNG; configurable time window\n10. **Fare tracking** — OMNY fare cap progress and weekly spending\n11. **Elevator/escalator status** — equipment status per station from the MTA ENE feed\n12. **Health dashboard** — `/status` HTML page and `/api/health` JSON endpoint with per-feed circuit-breaker state\n\n## Transit Data\n\nAll real-time data is from the [MTA GTFS-RT Protobuf API](https://api.mta.info/). An API key is optional but recommended to avoid rate limiting — register free at https://api.mta.info/\n\n| Feed | Lines |\n|------|-------|\n| gtfs | 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 S GS |\n| gtfs-ace | A C E H FS |\n| gtfs-bdfm | B D F M |\n| gtfs-g | G |\n| gtfs-jz | J Z |\n| gtfs-l | L |\n| gtfs-nqrw | N Q R W |\n| gtfs-si | SIR (Staten Island Railway) |\n\nPolling intervals: 30s (arrivals), 60s (alerts), 300s (equipment). Static schedule from MTA's published GTFS ZIP.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n**Prerequisites:** Node.js ≥ 22, npm ≥ 10\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way.git\ncd mta-my-way\nnpm install\n\n# One-time: process GTFS static schedule data\nnpm run process-gtfs --workspace=packages/server\n\n# Start development server\nnpm run dev --workspace=packages/server\n```\n\nApp available at `http://localhost:3001`. Set `MTA_API_KEY` in your environment to avoid MTA rate limits during development.\n\n## Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker build -t mta-my-way .\ndocker run \\\n  -e ALLOWED_HOSTS=your.domain.com \\\n  -e MTA_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\n  -p 3000:3000 \\\n  mta-my-way\n```\n\nThe container exposes port 3000.\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Notes |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------|\n| `PORT` | No | `3000` | HTTP listen port |\n| `NODE_ENV` | No | — | `production` or `development` |\n| `ALLOWED_HOSTS` | Yes (prod) | — | Comma-separated hostnames; server refuses to start without this in production |\n| `MTA_API_KEY` | Recommended | — | MTA GTFS-RT API key; works without but may be rate-limited. Register at https://api.mta.info/ |\n| `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY` | For push | — | Generate: `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys` |\n| `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` | For push | — | Never commit to version control |\n| `VAPID_SUBJECT` | For push | `mailto:mta-my-way@example.com` | Contact URI included in push requests |\n| `PASSWORD_PEPPER` | Recommended | — | `openssl rand -hex 32` |\n| `EMAIL_PROVIDER` | No | `console` | `ses`, `smtp`, `sendgrid`, or `console` |\n| `EMAIL_FROM` | No | `noreply@mtamyway.com` | Sender address for transactional email |\n| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `info` | `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |\n\n## Development Commands\n\n```bash\nnpm test                        # all unit/integration tests (Vitest)\nnpm run test:watch              # watch mode\ncd tests/e2e && npm test        # Playwright E2E tests\nnpm run lint                    # Biome + ESLint\nnpm run format                  # Biome format --write\nnpm run typecheck               # tsc --build\n```\n\n**Code style:** Biome for formatting, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Commit prefix convention: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`.\n\n## API Reference\n\n| Method | Path | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| GET | `/status` | Health dashboard (HTML) |\n| GET | `/api/health` | Per-feed status and circuit-breaker state (JSON) |\n| GET | `/api/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |\n| GET | `/api/arrivals/:stationId` | Real-time arrivals for a station |\n| GET | `/api/stations` | Full GTFS station list |\n| GET | `/api/stations/search` | Type-ahead search by name, line, or cross-street |\n| GET | `/api/routes` | Route index |\n| POST | `/api/commute/analyze` | Analyze routes between origin and destination |\n| GET | `/api/alerts` | All current MTA alerts |\n| GET | `/api/alerts/:lineId` | Alerts filtered by line |\n| GET | `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | VAPID public key for push registration |\n| POST | `/api/push/subscribe` | Register device for push notifications |\n| GET | `/api/trip/:tripId` | Live trip progress (stop-by-stop) |\n\n## Observability\n\nOpenTelemetry (OTLP gRPC), Prometheus `/api/metrics`, Pino structured JSON logging. See [docs/observability.md](docs/observability.md) for configuration details.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nmta-my-way/\n├── packages/\n│   ├── shared/           # TypeScript types and constants (feed config, polling intervals)\n│   ├── server/\n│   │   ├── src/          # Hono app, pollers, GTFS parsers, push engine, context engine\n│   │   ├── data/         # Generated GTFS JSON + SQLite (subscriptions, trips, sessions)\n│   │   └── scripts/      # process-gtfs.mjs\n│   └── web/\n│       └── src/\n│           ├── screens/  # Home, Station, Map, Commute, Alerts, Journal, Stats, ...\n│           ├── components/\n│           ├── hooks/\n│           └── stores/   # Zustand (favorites, journal, settings, fare)\n├── tests/\n│   └── e2e/              # Playwright tests\n├── docs/\n│   ├── plan/plan.md      # Architecture and roadmap\n│   ├── testing.md\n│   ├── security.md\n│   └── observability.md\n└── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: build-web → build-server → runtime\n```\n\n## Documentation\n\n- [Testing Guide](docs/testing.md)\n- [Security](docs/security.md)\n- [Observability](docs/observability.md)\n- [Architecture Plan](docs/plan/plan.md)\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","numLines":161,"startLine":1,"totalLines":161}}}

Load GTFS static data (~1-2s)\n52\t  //   2. Run migrations (~1s)\n53\t  //   3. Initialize subsystems (VAPID, OpenTelemetry, etc.)\n54\t  //   4. Start HTTP server (<1s) — health endpoint becomes available\n55\t  //   5. Start feed pollers in background (fire immediately but async)\n56\t  //\n57\t  // Port conflict detection:\n58\t  // Before starting the server, we check if port 3001 is already in use.\n59\t  // This prevents confusing \"port already in use\" errors during test runs.\n60\t  // The check script exits with code 1 if the port is busy, providing clear\n61\t  // guidance on how to resolve the conflict.\n62\t  //\n63\t  // Timeout (60s) accounts for typical server startup time:\n64\t  // - GTFS data loading and initialization (~1-2s)\n65\t  // - Database migrations (~1s)\n66\t  // - VAPID key generation (~1s)\n67\t  // - OpenTelemetry initialization (~1s)\n68\t  // - HTTP server start (<1s)\n69\t  //\n70\t  // The HTTP server starts BEFORE feed pollers fire, so the health endpoint\n71\t  // responds immediately once the database is reachable. Pollers run in the\n72\t  // background after the server is listening.\n73\t  //\n74\t  // Health-check polling & retry:\n75\t  // Playwright polls the `url` every ~500ms until it receives a 2xx response or\n76\t  // the `timeout` is reached.  If the server crashes during startup, Playwright\n77\t  // restarts the `command` and retries the health check — this is the built-in\n78\t  // retry mechanism.  We use the lightweight /health endpoint (registered before\n79\t  // all middleware in app.ts) so readiness checks respond in <1ms regardless of\n80\t  // rate-limit or CSRF state.  It returns 200 once the HTTP server is listening\n81\t  // and the database is reachable (SELECT 1), before feed pollers fire.\n82\t  //\n83\t  // reuseExistingServer:\n84\t  //   - CI (process.env.CI=true): Always start a fresh server for clean state\n85\t  //   - Local development (CI unset/false): Reuse existing dev server to avoid\n86\t  //     startup overhead. If no server is running, Playwright starts one.\n87\t  //   - To force a fresh server locally: CI=true npx playwright test\n88\t  //   - To reuse in CI: Not recommended (tests may share state), but possible\n89\t  //     by removing the CI env var in the workflow template.\n90\t  webServer: {\n91\t    command: \"npx tsx helpers/check-port.ts && cd ../.. && npx tsx packages/server/src/index.ts\",\n92\t    env: {\n93\t      TEST_MODE: \"true\",\n94\t    },\n95\t    url: \"http://localhost:3001/health\",\n96\t    reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,\n97\t    timeout: 60 * 1000,\n98\t  },\n99\t});\n100\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e14e0169-84cf-4b43-9dcc-5d36fa70a675","uuid":"9ef90953-a689-42ae-9a0f-292e5e62d7a8","timestamp":"2026-07-04T09:51:47.978Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts","content":"/**\n * Playwright E2E test configuration for MTA My Way.\n *\n * Tests run against a locally running server on http://localhost:3001\n */\n\nimport { defineConfig, devices } from \"@playwright/test\";\n\nexport default defineConfig({\n  testDir: \"./\",\n  testMatch: [\"**/*.e2e.ts\"],\n  fullyParallel: true,\n  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,\n  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,\n  workers: process.env.CI ? 1 : undefined,\n  reporter: \"html\",\n  use: {\n    baseURL: \"http://localhost:3001\",\n    trace: \"on-first-retry\",\n    screenshot: \"only-on-failure\",\n  },\n\n  projects: [\n    {\n      name: \"chromium\",\n      use: { ...devices[\"Desktop Chrome\"] },\n    },\n    {\n      name: \"firefox\",\n      use: { ...devices[\"Desktop Firefox\"] },\n    },\n    {\n      name: \"webkit\",\n      use: { ...devices[\"Desktop Safari\"] },\n    },\n    {\n      name: \"Mobile Chrome\",\n      use: { ...devices[\"Pixel 5\"] },\n    },\n    {\n      name: \"Mobile Safari\",\n      use: { ...devices[\"iPhone 12\"] },\n    },\n  ],\n\n  // Start the local dev server before running tests.\n  // Uses tsx to transpile TypeScript on-the-fly (avoids tsc -b build failures\n  // from unrelated type errors in the codebase).\n  //\n  // Server startup sequence (packages/server/src/index.ts):\n  //   1. Load GTFS static data (~1-2s)\n  //   2. Run migrations (~1s)\n  //   3. Initialize subsystems (VAPID, OpenTelemetry, etc.)\n  //   4. Start HTTP server (<1s) — health endpoint becomes available\n  //   5. Start feed pollers in background (fire immediately but async)\n  //\n  // Port conflict detection:\n  // Before starting the server, we check if port 3001 is already in use.\n  // This prevents confusing \"port already in use\" errors during test runs.\n  // The check script exits with code 1 if the port is busy, providing clear\n  // guidance on how to resolve the conflict.\n  //\n  // Timeout (60s) accounts for typical server startup time:\n  // - GTFS data loading and initialization (~1-2s)\n  // - Database migrations (~1s)\n  // - VAPID key generation (~1s)\n  // - OpenTelemetry initialization (~1s)\n  // - HTTP server start (<1s)\n  //\n  // The HTTP server starts BEFORE feed pollers fire, so the health endpoint\n  // responds immediately once the database is reachable. Pollers run in the\n  // background after the server is listening.\n  //\n  // Health-check polling & retry:\n  // Playwright polls the `url` every ~500ms until it receives a 2xx response or\n  // the `timeout` is reached.  If the server crashes during startup, Playwright\n  // restarts the `command` and retries the health check — this is the built-in\n  // retry mechanism.  We use the lightweight /health endpoint (registered before\n  // all middleware in app.ts) so readiness checks respond in <1ms regardless of\n  // rate-limit or CSRF state. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e14e0169-84cf-4b43-9dcc-5d36fa70a675","uuid":"b9ec5d5b-bd7c-475f-b185-8a0710e85ad2","timestamp":"2026-07-04T09:54:33.486Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/README.md","oldString":"## Preview\n\nScreenshots are available in `docs/`.","newString":"## Preview\n\n![Home Dashboard](docs/screenshots/home-dashboard.png)\n\n![Station Detail](docs/screenshots/station-detail.png)\n\n![Commute Planner](docs/screenshots/commute-planner.png)\n\n![Interactive Map](docs/screenshots/interactive-map.png)","originalFile":"# MTA My Way\n\nA mobile-first Progressive Web App (PWA) for daily NYC subway commuters. Design philosophy: **open and see your data in under three seconds** — your pinned stations front and center, no map to dismiss, no search to type.\n\nVersion 0.0.1\n\n## Preview\n\nScreenshots are available in `docs/`.\n\n## Features\n\n1. **Favorites-first home screen** — pinned stations with inline live arrival countdowns; GPS-powered 60-second onboarding for first-time users\n2. **Real-time arrivals** — live countdowns per station, both directions; pull-to-refresh; 30s auto-refresh; offline fallback via PWA cache\n3. **Transfer intelligence** — commute analyzer shows whether you should transfer using real-time train positions: RECOMMENDED / DIRECT / ALSO POSSIBLE, with walk-time comparison\n4. **Predictive delay detection** — tracks vehicle positions across consecutive 30s polls; when a train's inter-station time exceeds 2× the scheduled baseline, generates early-warning synthetic alerts before MTA publishes them\n5. **Smart alerts** — filtered to your exact stations, lines, and directions; plain-language rewrites of raw MTA alert text; push notifications via WebPush/VAPID\n6. **Context-aware switching** — detects home/commute/transfer context from location, time patterns, and tap history; frequency-sorts favorites during commute hours\n7. **Interactive transit map** — SVG map with pan/zoom, real-time pulsing train positions, line filtering, and tap-to-detail modal\n8. **Trip journal** — automatic trip tracking with full history and statistics (average, median, std dev, trends)\n9. **Subway Year** — shareable annual summary card (Spotify Wrapped-style), exportable as PNG; configurable time window\n10. **Fare tracking** — OMNY fare cap progress and weekly spending\n11. **Elevator/escalator status** — equipment status per station from the MTA ENE feed\n12. **Health dashboard** — `/status` HTML page and `/api/health` JSON endpoint with per-feed circuit-breaker state\n\n## Transit Data\n\nAll real-time data is from the [MTA GTFS-RT Protobuf API](https://api.mta.info/). An API key is optional but recommended to avoid rate limiting — register free at https://api.mta.info/\n\n| Feed | Lines |\n|------|-------|\n| gtfs | 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 S GS |\n| gtfs-ace | A C E H FS |\n| gtfs-bdfm | B D F M |\n| gtfs-g | G |\n| gtfs-jz | J Z |\n| gtfs-l | L |\n| gtfs-nqrw | N Q R W |\n| gtfs-si | SIR (Staten Island Railway) |\n\nPolling intervals: 30s (arrivals), 60s (alerts), 300s (equipment). Static schedule from MTA's published GTFS ZIP.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n**Prerequisites:** Node.js ≥ 22, npm ≥ 10\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way.git\ncd mta-my-way\nnpm install\n\n# One-time: process GTFS static schedule data\nnpm run process-gtfs --workspace=packages/server\n\n# Start development server\nnpm run dev --workspace=packages/server\n```\n\nApp available at `http://localhost:3001`. Set `MTA_API_KEY` in your environment to avoid MTA rate limits during development.\n\n## Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker build -t mta-my-way .\ndocker run \\\n  -e ALLOWED_HOSTS=your.domain.com \\\n  -e MTA_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\n  -p 3000:3000 \\\n  mta-my-way\n```\n\nThe container exposes port 3000.\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Notes |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------|\n| `PORT` | No | `3000` | HTTP listen port |\n| `NODE_ENV` | No | — | `production` or `development` |\n| `ALLOWED_HOSTS` | Yes (prod) | — | Comma-separated hostnames; server refuses to start without this in production |\n| `MTA_API_KEY` | Recommended | — | MTA GTFS-RT API key; works without but may be rate-limited. Register at https://api.mta.info/ |\n| `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY` | For push | — | Generate: `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys` |\n| `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` | For push | — | Never commit to version control |\n| `VAPID_SUBJECT` | For push | `mailto:mta-my-way@example.com` | Contact URI included in push requests |\n| `PASSWORD_PEPPER` | Recommended | — | `openssl rand -hex 32` |\n| `EMAIL_PROVIDER` | No | `console` | `ses`, `smtp`, `sendgrid`, or `console` |\n| `EMAIL_FROM` | No | `noreply@mtamyway.com` | Sender address for transactional email |\n| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `info` | `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |\n\n## Development Commands\n\n```bash\nnpm test                        # all unit/integration tests (Vitest)\nnpm run test:watch              # watch mode\ncd tests/e2e && npm test        # Playwright E2E tests\nnpm run lint                    # Biome + ESLint\nnpm run format                  # Biome format --write\nnpm run typecheck               # tsc --build\n```\n\n**Code style:** Biome for formatting, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Commit prefix convention: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`.\n\n## API Reference\n\n| Method | Path | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| GET | `/status` | Health dashboard (HTML) |\n| GET | `/api/health` | Per-feed status and circuit-breaker state (JSON) |\n| GET | `/api/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |\n| GET | `/api/arrivals/:stationId` | Real-time arrivals for a station |\n| GET | `/api/stations` | Full GTFS station list |\n| GET | `/api/stations/search` | Type-ahead search by name, line, or cross-street |\n| GET | `/api/routes` | Route index |\n| POST | `/api/commute/analyze` | Analyze routes between origin and destination |\n| GET | `/api/alerts` | All current MTA alerts |\n| GET | `/api/alerts/:lineId` | Alerts filtered by line |\n| GET | `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | VAPID public key for push registration |\n| POST | `/api/push/subscribe` | Register device for push notifications |\n| GET | `/api/trip/:tripId` | Live trip progress (stop-by-stop) |\n\n## Observability\n\nOpenTelemetry (OTLP gRPC), Prometheus `/api/metrics`, Pino structured JSON logging. See [docs/observability.md](docs/observability.md) for configuration details.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nmta-my-way/\n├── packages/\n│   ├── shared/           # TypeScript types and constants (feed config, polling intervals)\n│   ├── server/\n│   │   ├── src/          # Hono app, pollers, GTFS parsers, push engine, context engine\n│   │   ├── data/         # Generated GTFS JSON + SQLite (subscriptions, trips, sessions)\n│   │   └── scripts/      # process-gtfs.mjs\n│   └── web/\n│       └── src/\n│           ├── screens/  # Home, Station, Map, Commute, Alerts, Journal, Stats, ...\n│           ├── components/\n│           ├── hooks/\n│           └── stores/   # Zustand (favorites, journal, settings, fare)\n├── tests/\n│   └── e2e/              # Playwright tests\n├── docs/\n│   ├── plan/plan.md      # Architecture and roadmap\n│   ├── testing.md\n│   ├── security.md\n│   └── observability.md\n└── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: build-web → build-server → runtime\n```\n\n## Documentation\n\n- [Testing Guide](docs/testing.md)\n- [Security](docs/security.md)\n- [Observability](docs/observability.md)\n- [Architecture Plan](docs/plan/plan.md)\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":6,"oldLines":8,"newStart":6,"newLines":14,"lines":[" "," ## Preview"," ","-Screenshots are available in `docs/`.","+![Home Dashboard](docs/screenshots/home-dashboard.png)"," ","+![Station Detail](docs/screenshots/station-detail.png)","+","+![Commute Planner](docs/screenshots/commute-planner.png)","+","+![Interactive Map](docs/screenshots/interactive-map.png)","+"," ## Features"," "," 1. **Favorites-first home screen** — pinned stations with inline live arrival countdowns; GPS-powered 60-second onboarding for first-time users"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
